questions: Does any library module or library-related code exist in the CLI?
What module and routing patterns must the new library module follow?
How does item reference resolution work, and what parallel is needed for library items?
Does SKILL.md need a Library section for agent discoverability?
How does the continue command integrate with library comment context?

answers.answer: No library module or library-related code exists in the CLI. src/index.ts switch-case dispatcher (lines 72-124) has no 'library' case. No src/library/ directory exists.
evidence: src/index.ts: Switch cases at lines 72-124 include login, token, inspect, comments, org, tickets, skill, update — no library
No src/library/ directory in the repository

answers.answer: The CLI uses a consistent module pattern: src/{domain}/index.ts exports a runX(config, args) function that dispatches to cmd* functions based on subcommand. The comments module (src/comments/index.ts) is the closest parallel — it dispatches to cmdCommentsList and cmdCommentsPost. The library module needs: index.ts (router), list.ts, show.ts, comments.ts (nested router), comments-list.ts, comments-post.ts.
evidence: src/comments/index.ts: runComments dispatches to cmdCommentsList and cmdCommentsPost
src/index.ts: Each top-level command calls runX(config, args.slice(1))
src/lib/flags.ts: getFlag(), hasFlag(), getPositionalArgs() for argument parsing

answers.answer: Ticket resolution (src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts) supports internal ID, short ID (XXX-NNN pattern), and numeric ticket number via extractTicketRef() and matchTicket(). Library item resolution needs a parallel resolve-library-item.ts supporting cuid, ticket short ID (find latest library item for that ticket), and title substring match. The API endpoint for resolution is GET /api/library/items (already exists in the server).
evidence: src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts: extractTicketRef extracts ref from --ticket flag or positional args
src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts: matchTicket matches by exact ID, shortId, or numeric ticket number

answers.answer: Yes. SKILL.md (skill-content/SKILL.md, 148 lines) documents all CLI commands for agent discoverability. It has sections for Available Commands, Common Workflows, Flag Conventions, but no Library section. Adding a Library section is critical for agents to know about library list, show, comments list, and comments post commands.
evidence: skill-content/SKILL.md: 148 lines with sections for commands, workflows, guardrails — no Library section

answers.answer: The continue command (src/tickets/continue.ts) posts continuationContext to POST /api/tickets/{ticketId}/rerun. Currently requires non-empty context text. The research report specifies that library comments should be auto-included in continuation context server-side. The CLI continue command does not need to change for MVP — the server assembles library comments into the context. However, SKILL.md should document the workflow of posting library comments before continuing.
evidence: Continuation integration is primarily server-side (auto-fetch library comments during rerun)

statement: Complete CLI feature gap: no library module exists. A new src/library/ module must be created with list, show, comments list, and comments post commands following the established module pattern (switch-case routing, flag parsing, hxFetch HTTP client). A resolve-library-item.ts utility parallels resolve-ticket.ts. SKILL.md needs a Library section for agent discoverability. The continue command does not need changes for MVP since continuation integration is server-side.
